title: As World Shuts Borders to Stop Omicron, Japan Offers a Cautionary Tale
content: Japan, which has been very cautious throughout the pandemic, is again barring all nonresident foreigners. There is an economic and human cost.

more_info.byline: By Motoko Rich and Hikari Hida
geo_facet: Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

en_title: As World Shuts Borders to Stop Omicron, Japan Offers a Cautionary Tale
en_content: Japan, which has been very cautious throughout the pandemic, is again barring all nonresident foreigners. There is an economic and human cost.

title: Will the Vaccines Stop Omicron? Scientists Are Racing to Find Out.
content: A “Frankenstein mix” of mutations raises concerns, but the variant may remain vulnerable to current vaccines. If not, revisions will be necessary.
en_title: Will the Vaccines Stop Omicron? Scientists Are Racing to Find Out.
en_content: A “Frankenstein mix” of mutations raises concerns, but the variant may remain vulnerable to current vaccines. If not, revisions will be necessary.

title: Honduras Election Front-Runner Vows New Era but Is Tied to Past
content: Xiomara Castro, headed toward becoming her country’s next president, promises to expunge its legacy of corruption, but change may be tempered by her establishment ties and conservative opposition.

more_info.byline: By Anatoly Kurmanaev and Joan Suazo

en_title: Honduras Election Front-Runner Vows New Era but Is Tied to Past
en_content: Xiomara Castro, headed toward becoming her country’s next president, promises to expunge its legacy of corruption, but change may be tempered by her establishment ties and conservative opposition.

title: Iran Insists on Immediate Lifting of Sanctions as Nuclear Talks Resume
content: After five months, negotiations on restoring the 2015 nuclear deal with Iran resumed in Vienna, but with a harder line from the new Iranian government.

des_facet: Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

en_title: Iran Insists on Immediate Lifting of Sanctions as Nuclear Talks Resume
en_content: After five months, negotiations on restoring the 2015 nuclear deal with Iran resumed in Vienna, but with a harder line from the new Iranian government.

title: She Fled North Korea for Freedom. Then She Was Arrested.
content: The case of “Chrysanthemum” offers a rare look at the covert battle the rival Koreas are waging over North Korean defectors living in the South.

org_facet: Ministry of State Security of the People's Republic of China

en_title: She Fled North Korea for Freedom. Then She Was Arrested.
en_content: The case of “Chrysanthemum” offers a rare look at the covert battle the rival Koreas are waging over North Korean defectors living in the South.

title: Sweden Elects Its First Female Leader — for Second Time in a Week
content: The Swedish Parliament elected Magdalena Andersson as prime minister by a narrow margin. She was first elected last week, but her government soon collapsed over a budget dispute.

more_info.byline: By Cora Engelbrecht and Christina Anderson

en_title: Sweden Elects Its First Female Leader — for Second Time in a Week
en_content: The Swedish Parliament elected Magdalena Andersson as prime minister by a narrow margin. She was first elected last week, but her government soon collapsed over a budget dispute.

title: In a Nonbinary Pronoun, France Sees a U.S. Attack on the Republic
content: When a French dictionary included the gender-nonspecific “iel” for the first time, a virulent reaction erupted over “wokisme” exported from American universities.

more_info.byline: By Roger Cohen and Léontine Gallois
des_facet: Le Petit Robert de la Langue Francaise (Book)

en_title: In a Nonbinary Pronoun, France Sees a U.S. Attack on the Republic
en_content: When a French dictionary included the gender-nonspecific “iel” for the first time, a virulent reaction erupted over “wokisme” exported from American universities.

title: As U.S. Hunts for Chinese Spies, University Scientists Warn of Backlash
content: A chilling effect has taken hold on American campuses, contributing to an outflow of academic talent that may hurt the United States while benefiting Beijing.
en_title: As U.S. Hunts for Chinese Spies, University Scientists Warn of Backlash
en_content: A chilling effect has taken hold on American campuses, contributing to an outflow of academic talent that may hurt the United States while benefiting Beijing.

title: Hunt for the ‘Blood Diamond of Batteries’ Impedes Green Energy Push
content: Dangerous mining conditions plague Congo, home to the world’s largest supply of cobalt, a key ingredient in electric cars. A leadership battle threatens reforms.

more_info.byline: By Dionne Searcey, Eric Lipton and Ashley Gilbertson

en_title: Hunt for the ‘Blood Diamond of Batteries’ Impedes Green Energy Push
en_content: Dangerous mining conditions plague Congo, home to the world’s largest supply of cobalt, a key ingredient in electric cars. A leadership battle threatens reforms.

title: A ‘Simpsons’ Episode Lampooned Chinese Censorship. In Hong Kong, It Vanished.
content: The episode mocked both Mao Zedong and the government’s efforts to suppress memory of the 1989 Tiananmen Square massacre.
en_title: A ‘Simpsons’ Episode Lampooned Chinese Censorship. In Hong Kong, It Vanished.
en_content: The episode mocked both Mao Zedong and the government’s efforts to suppress memory of the 1989 Tiananmen Square massacre.

title: Climate Change Is Driving Some Albatrosses to ‘Divorce,’ Study Finds
content: Warming oceans are sending the monogamous sea birds farther afield to find food, putting stress on their breeding and prompting some to ditch their partners.
en_title: Climate Change Is Driving Some Albatrosses to ‘Divorce,’ Study Finds
en_content: Warming oceans are sending the monogamous sea birds farther afield to find food, putting stress on their breeding and prompting some to ditch their partners.

title: ‘You’re Not Helpless’: For London Women, Learning to Fight Builds Confidence
content: After a year marked by isolation, loneliness and violence in the city, many self-defense and martial arts gyms say they are seeing more interest from women.
en_title: ‘You’re Not Helpless’: For London Women, Learning to Fight Builds Confidence
en_content: After a year marked by isolation, loneliness and violence in the city, many self-defense and martial arts gyms say they are seeing more interest from women.

title: A Wine Rack on Rails? U.K. Businesses Seek Solutions to Shortages.
content: Two months after concerns about gas and food stocks caused ripples of anxiety, Britain continues to face problems in its supply chain. Distributors and retailers are looking for creative fixes.

more_info.byline: By Stephen Castle and Jenny Gross

en_title: A Wine Rack on Rails? U.K. Businesses Seek Solutions to Shortages.
en_content: Two months after concerns about gas and food stocks caused ripples of anxiety, Britain continues to face problems in its supply chain. Distributors and retailers are looking for creative fixes.

title: Flood-Drenched British Columbians Fear This Is the New Normal
content: Washed-out roads and destroyed bridges are just some of the devastation afflicting residents of the western province and could signal what climate change will bring in the future.
en_title: Flood-Drenched British Columbians Fear This Is the New Normal
en_content: Washed-out roads and destroyed bridges are just some of the devastation afflicting residents of the western province and could signal what climate change will bring in the future.

title: ‘No red flags’ yet: South African scientists caution against panic over the new variant.
content: Officials said that while they need more data to be sure, existing treatments and precautions seem to be effective against the Omicron variant.
en_title: ‘No red flags’ yet: South African scientists caution against panic over the new variant.
en_content: Officials said that while they need more data to be sure, existing treatments and precautions seem to be effective against the Omicron variant.

title: The U.K. announces Omicron cases and extends boosters to all adults.
content: The British government was widely criticized for a sluggish response to the Delta variant, and its reaction to Omicron came markedly quicker.

more_info.byline: By Megan Specia and Cora Engelbrecht

en_title: The U.K. announces Omicron cases and extends boosters to all adults.
en_content: The British government was widely criticized for a sluggish response to the Delta variant, and its reaction to Omicron came markedly quicker.

title: Dutch police arrest a couple who fled quarantine and boarded a plane.
content: One of the pair tested positive for the coronavirus after they arrived on a flight from South Africa. They were quarantined at a hotel.
en_title: Dutch police arrest a couple who fled quarantine and boarded a plane.
en_content: One of the pair tested positive for the coronavirus after they arrived on a flight from South Africa. They were quarantined at a hotel.

title: Czech president appoints a new prime minister — from inside a transparent cube.
content: President Milos Zeman tested positive for the coronavirus on Thursday and went into isolation, delaying the ceremony for three days.

title: Portugal detects 13 cases of Omicron tied to a soccer team.
content: The team, Belenenses, took part in a game that had to be abandoned after a Covid outbreak reduced the number of eligible players. One infected player had recently returned from South Africa.
